Claim: The pipeline is platform-neutral because it depends only on the DiscussionContext/CommentContext Protocol, not on Open edX types (ADR 0011) - this is the core 'vendor-neutral' thesis claim.
Test Setup: Implement a second, minimal Protocol-conforming backend (even a toy/synthetic one, distinct from StubLMSBackend).
Test Description: Run the pipeline unmodified against this second backend.
Expected Result: Pipeline completes with no code changes, confirming no pipeline code references Open edX-specific fields.
Comments: New from ADR 0011 review (2026-07-20). Without this, 'vendor-neutral' is only tested against the one platform it was built for.

Claim: StubLMSBackend and OpenEdXBackend implement the same interface such that swapping is a pure configuration change with identical pipeline decisions (ADR 0011/ADR 0022).
Test Setup: Equivalent thread content run through both backends.
Test Description: Compare classification/role/technique output between StubLMSBackend and a controlled real or mocked Open edX instance.
Expected Result: Identical pipeline decisions across backends for the same input.
Comments: New from ADR 0011/0022 review (2026-07-20). Complements A01 with the two backends that already exist.

Claim: Model compatibility must be established for every model in the eval roster - vendor-neutral claim extended across LLM providers.
Test Setup: Add openrouter:meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free to a scratch .env.openrouter run.
Test Description: Run all 8 ADR-0023 fixtures against this model in one Trigger/eval-models pass.
Expected Result: Completes all 8, establishing a first coverage baseline (never tested before).
Comments: Free-tier rate limiting may require EVAL_DELAY_SECONDS spacing.

Claim: The heterogeneous multi-model pipeline actually invokes distinct configured models per stage (Settings.model_for(agent), ADR 0019), not just one model reused everywhere.
Test Setup: Configure distinct models per stage (e.g. a cheap classifier model / an expensive role-agent model).
Test Description: Run a fixture and inspect logfire spans (or instrument model_for) to confirm each stage's LLM call targets its configured model.
Expected Result: Each of the 4 pipeline stages calls its own configured model.
Comments: New from ADR 0019 review (2026-07-20). Directly tests the 'multi-model pipeline' part of the architecture claim.

Claim: qwen2.5:14b in prompted-extraction mode must not regress to the Ollama null-content 400 error, even after a validation retry triggered by a more complex schema (ADR 0012, documented historical bug).
Test Setup: Force a validation-retry scenario (e.g. via pydantic-ai FunctionModel) against qwen2.5:14b's profile, with a schema that includes the confidence field.
Test Description: Run the forced-retry scenario and check for the `invalid message content type: <nil>` error.
Expected Result: No null-content error; retry completes with a valid instance.
Comments: New from ADR 0012 review (2026-07-20) - a specific documented historical bug not covered by general model-compatibility onboarding (A03).

Claim: FACILITATION_STALLED_THRESHOLD_HOURS is live-wired into the classifier prompt and actually changes classification behavior, not just documented (ADR 0018).
Test Setup: Same fixture run with two different threshold values.
Test Description: Run the stalled_thread fixture once per threshold value.
Expected Result: The `stalled` label flips accordingly when the threshold crosses the thread's actual inactivity duration.
Comments: New from ADR 0018 review (2026-07-20).

Claim: ClassifierEval/ResponseEval add zero extra cost when disabled via config - no rule checks, no LLM call (ADR 0005).
Test Setup: Two runs of the same fixture: eval flags off vs. on.
Test Description: Instrument or mock the agent-call count for each run.
Expected Result: Zero additional agent invocations when off; exactly one extra call per eval stage when on.
Comments: New from ADR 0005 review (2026-07-20).

Claim: InMemoryThreadStore and SQLiteThreadStore are interchangeable without pipeline code changes (ADR 0007) - needed for cooldown/EMT history to be a reliable dependency regardless of deployment backend.
Test Setup: The same scenario (fixture + seeded prior intervention) run against both store implementations.
Test Description: Compare cooldown/escalation decisions between the two backends.
Expected Result: Identical decisions across both store implementations.
Comments: New from ADR 0007 review (2026-07-20). Same shape as Q04 (S3/filesystem parity) but for the history store.

Claim: The pipeline's graph control flow (node routing, retry loop) and graceful degradation (ADR 0032) must produce documented, non-crashing behavior at every node under real fault conditions - not just under the exceptions they were designed for.
Test Setup: Needs Q07's TestModel-based graph test as a base, then fault injection: mock a timeout, a malformed-schema exception, and a network error at Classification/Intervention/Orchestrator/Role nodes.
Test Description: Write and run fault-injection tests; also construct a scenario engineered to fail rule checks repeatably (e.g. forcing evaluative language) to observe the exhausted-max_orchestrator_retries path.
Expected Result: Each injected failure produces the documented partial PipelineResult, not a crash; exhausted-retry path is documented and non-crashing.
Comments: Merges RQ8 and RQ9. Classification is expected to NOT degrade gracefully by design (ADR 0032 - no useful data exists if it fails); confirm that asymmetry holds too.

Claim: Tool-call plumbing reaches real backend implementations, not just prompt-wiring - quantifies how much of the system's failure surface is integration bugs vs. model output quality.
Test Setup: overt_attack.json probe (docs/threads/overt_attack.json), run via the playground.
Test Description: `uv run --env-file .env facilitate docs/threads/overt_attack.json --pretty`, then audit how many of the 5 role agents' tool-registered functions have direct test coverage at the tool-call level (not just prompt-wiring), following the Q06 flag_content precedent.
Expected Result: Moderator selected; flag_content succeeds against a real or stub backend; post_to_thread=False on escalation; audit produces a clear inventory of tested vs. untested tools.
Comments: Merges the old 'overt attack' fixture test, Q06, and RQ14. Tension #4 (any role can call instructor_escalation, but only moderator has flag_content) - see B23 for the undetected-mismatch case this doesn't cover. Fixed 2026-07-22: this file had the same children/body/author schema bug as B13-B16 (silently loaded as an empty thread) - not previously run against real content.

Claim: Structural response assertions (ADR 0020 Layer 1) are directly unit tested for every invariant.
Test Setup: See Q08.
Test Description: See Q08.
Expected Result: See Q08.
Comments: Duplicate of Q08 - listed here because it's also load-bearing for the architecture-feasibility claim (Layer 1 is the floor the whole evaluation framework stands on).

Claim: Closed threads must not be classified, intervened upon, or responded to, from any entry point.
Test Setup: Needs a thread fixture with closed: true (does not exist yet).
Test Description: Run the closed thread through POST /facilitate and the eval runner directly (not /runs/live/trigger, which already guards this per test_trigger_live_run_closed_thread_returns_noop).
Expected Result: Pipeline refuses to run, or the guard is confirmed missing and logged as such.
Comments: Corrected 2026-07-20: earlier claim 'no guard exists anywhere' was stale - router.py:733 already guards the live-trigger path specifically.

Claim: The full facilitation pipeline must work end-to-end against real, non-synthetic Open edX threads.
Test Setup: A live Open edX course with real discussion threads; valid JWT.
Test Description: Trigger a Live course run (Trigger UI, Live course source), once per environment: idril, EC2.
Expected Result: Full pipeline completes against real thread content and structure on each environment.
Comments: Only one historical run exists (2026-06-14), predates the clean slate and this session's pipeline/router changes. See Q13 for the server-side JWT TTL prerequisite and Q09 for the dry-run negative path.

Claim: Real MOOC-extracted threads (docs/threads/real/, ADR 0041) and their synthetic AI-ethics equivalents (docs/threads/ai-ethics/) covering the same six patterns must produce comparable classification/role behavior across a different academic domain and register.
Test Setup: docs/threads/real/{dominated,explicit_distress,formulaic,hostile_then_silent,integration_phase,overt_attack}.json - a Java-programming-MOOC corpus, curated from the Zenodo Ruipérez-Valiente et al. (2021) dataset - vs. their AI-ethics synthetic counterparts already in the 8-fixture set and docs/threads/ai-ethics/.
Test Description: Run the full model roster against both corpora for each of the 6 shared patterns, compare classification/role selection.
Expected Result: Behavior is comparable across domain/register (real programming-MOOC language vs. synthetic AI-ethics language) for the same intended pattern, or divergence is characterized.
Comments: New from ADR 0041 review (2026-07-20) - answers RQ10 (domain generalization) and partially RQ11 (message-count shortcut) with fixtures that ALREADY EXIST. Previously RQ10/RQ11 were listed as blocked on new fixtures (see A21/A22); this reclassifies them as ready to run.

Claim: New thread with no comments must receive an organizational/social intervention.
Test Setup: Fixture `new_thread` (evals/fixtures/threads.py, ADR 0023).
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard (Sample data source) or `eval-models`, once per environment: local (10 Ollama models), idril (ministral-3:14b, qwen3.5:27b), EC2 (gpt-4o).
Expected Result: state=NEW, trajectory=never_started; should_intervene=True; role is organizational or social.
Comments: Historical local evidence exists (docs/experiments/results/) but predates this session's code changes and the idril/EC2 clean slate - treat as unverified until re-run.

Claim: An actively progressing thread must NOT receive an intervention.
Test Setup: Fixture `active_thread`.
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard or eval-models, once per environment: local, idril, EC2.
Expected Result: state=ACTIVE, trajectory=growing; should_intervene=False.
Comments: Historical local evidence exists but predates this session's code changes and the idril/EC2 clean slate.

Claim: A thread inactive beyond the 48h threshold (ADR 0018) must receive a re-engagement intervention.
Test Setup: Fixture `stalled_thread`.
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard or eval-models, once per environment.
Expected Result: state=STALLED, trajectory=declining; should_intervene=True; role among social/intellectual/organizational.
Comments: See A06 for the threshold-wiring test this decision depends on.

Claim: A thread with aggressive/dismissive exchange must receive de-escalation, without jumping straight to moderator.
Test Setup: Fixture `conflictive_thread`.
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard or eval-models, once per environment.
Expected Result: state=CONFLICTIVE; should_intervene=True; role among social/moderator/affective; moderator not selected as first resort.
Comments: Feeds into B12 (RQ4 moderator-as-last-resort) as one of several data points.

Claim: A thread that reached consensus must NOT receive an intervention.
Test Setup: Fixture `convergent_thread`.
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard or eval-models, once per environment.
Expected Result: state=CONVERGENT, trajectory=stable; should_intervene=False.
Comments: Historical local evidence exists but predates this session's code changes and the idril/EC2 clean slate.

Claim: A thread drifting from the stated learning objectives must be redirected.
Test Setup: Fixture `off_topic_thread`.
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard or eval-models, once per environment.
Expected Result: state=OFF_TOPIC; should_intervene=True; role is organizational.
Comments: See B21 for the dual-state (stalled+off_topic) priority gap this doesn't cover.

Claim: Formulaic discourse (no argument development) must trigger an intellectual intervention even though the thread is ACTIVE.
Test Setup: Fixture `shallow_discourse_thread`.
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard or eval-models, once per environment.
Expected Result: should_intervene=True with intellectual role; trigger is discourse_quality=formulaic, not activity level.
Comments: Distinct from B13 (formulaic.json probe) - this fixture isolates discourse_quality as the signal; B13 isolates technique selection given that signal.

Claim: Unbalanced participation must trigger a social intervention, driven by participation_balance, not state.
Test Setup: Fixture `dominated_thread`.
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard or eval-models, once per environment.
Expected Result: should_intervene=True with social role; trigger is participation_balance=dominated.
Comments: One of two independent data points for B10 (RQ2) - the other is the dominated.json design-tension probe.

Claim: RQ1 - Does trajectory information (declining vs. stable) actually change role selection or response content?
Test Setup: Two variants of the same state=stalled fixture, one with trajectory=declining (existing stalled_thread) and one constructed with trajectory=stable content.
Test Description: Run both through the pipeline, compare role selection and response content.
Expected Result: If trajectory matters, the two variants should differ in role/technique/tone despite the same state label.
Comments: See docs/experiments/research-questions.md for full literature/ADR grounding.

Claim: RQ2 - Does participation_balance ever reach the orchestrator as a structured signal, or does role selection happen on state alone?
Test Setup: B08's dominated_thread fixture plus the independent docs/threads/dominated.json design-tension probe, both already suggesting state-alone selection.
Test Description: Instrument/log the orchestrator's actual prompt input across a larger set of dominated-type runs.
Expected Result: Confirm whether participation_balance/discourse_quality/inquiry_phase ever appear outside classification_reasoning free text.
Comments: See docs/experiments/research-questions.md for full literature/ADR grounding.

Claim: RQ3 - Does the 'one action per intervention' constraint (ADR 0003, Phase 3) actually hold, or do responses drift toward combining actions?
Test Setup: Needs writing: a rule check (or LLM-judge rubric dimension) for single-action responses - does not exist yet.
Test Description: Write the check, run it against existing and newly generated responses.
Expected Result: Responses combine at most one action; violations are flagged.
Comments: See docs/experiments/research-questions.md for full literature/ADR grounding.

Claim: RQ4 - Does the moderator role actually behave as 'last resort,' or does the orchestrator reach for it on the first attempt?
Test Setup: B04's conflictive_thread and the hostile_then_silent.json design-tension probe, repeated across more conflictive-state fixtures and models.
Test Description: Run conflictive_thread and hostile_then_silent.json across the full model roster, tally first-role-selected.
Expected Result: Moderator is rarely/never the first role selected on a conflictive thread, across models.
Comments: See docs/experiments/research-questions.md for full literature/ADR grounding.

Claim: Tensions #1 (technique drift across the 30-technique repertoire) and #7 (list-order primacy bias). Intellectual role should select an EMT L1 pump-level technique, not drift to an unrelated one.
Test Setup: docs/threads/formulaic.json, run via the playground.
Test Description: `uv run --env-file .env facilitate docs/threads/formulaic.json --pretty`. Read output per docs/experiments.md's 'How to read the playground output'.
Expected Result: Intellectual role selected; technique consistent with the intellectual archetype and, ideally, an early-EMT-level pump.
Comments: Fixed 2026-07-22: the file was in raw Open edX API shape (children, no body/author), so DiscussionThread.model_validate() silently loaded it as an empty thread (extra fields are ignored by default). Any historical result from 2026-04-08 predates this fix and reflects title-only classification, not the intended tension - discard it, don't compare against it.

Claim: Tension #8 (forced single-label state suppresses a thread that is simultaneously stalled and conflictive) and #12 (moderator should not be first resort).
Test Setup: docs/threads/hostile_then_silent.json, run via the playground.
Test Description: `uv run --env-file .env facilitate docs/threads/hostile_then_silent.json --pretty`.
Expected Result: A defensible single state is chosen; moderator is not selected on the first attempt for a conflictive-adjacent thread.
Comments: Second data point for B12 (RQ4). Fixed 2026-07-22 (same children/body/author schema bug as B13) - discard the 2026-04-08 historical result, it never saw the thread content.

Claim: Tension #2 (do classification sub-dimensions reach the intervention agent).
Test Setup: docs/threads/integration_phase.json, run via the playground.
Test Description: `uv run --env-file .env facilitate docs/threads/integration_phase.json --pretty`.
Expected Result: should_intervene=False; inquiry_phase=resolution correctly informs the no-intervention decision.
Comments: Fixed 2026-07-22 (same children/body/author schema bug as B13) - discard the 2026-04-08 historical result, it never saw the thread content.

Claim: Tension #7 (affective techniques appear late in the 30-technique list; does the role still reach them).
Test Setup: docs/threads/explicit_distress.json, run via the playground.
Test Description: `uv run --env-file .env facilitate docs/threads/explicit_distress.json --pretty`.
Expected Result: Affective role selected with a technique from its own archetype (e.g. emotional_support).
Comments: Fixed 2026-07-22 (same children/body/author schema bug as B13) - discard the 2026-04-08 historical result, it never saw the thread content.

Claim: Cooldown/EMT escalation logic must avoid repeating a technique that produced no progress, across all roles - not only the intellectual EMT ladder (ADR 0046 §6.5, ADR 0007 principle).
Test Setup: Needs a harness to pre-populate history_store before the run (does not exist yet). Extend beyond intellectual: seed prior interventions of increasing intensity for other roles too.
Test Description: Build the harness, then run a thread twice with a seeded prior intervention record and compare technique selection, for at least one non-intellectual role.
Expected Result: Role agent avoids repeating the seeded prior technique, or explicitly escalates - and never resets to a milder level without cause, for any role, not just intellectual.
Comments: Tension #5, tracked in docs/TODO.md. Scope widened 2026-07-20 - ADR 0046's escalation ladder is described as applying across roles, not only intellectual/EMT.

Claim: Social role must prefer targeting a participant with a declining trajectory over one who never participated (Kim et al., 2021, per ADR 0004).
Test Setup: Fixture `declining_vs_never_posted` (evals/fixtures/threads.py, added 2026-07-22, source=synthetic).
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard (Sample data source, key `declining_vs_never_posted`) or `eval-models`, then check who the response addresses.
Expected Result: Response targets the declining participant (Quinn), not the never-posted one (Riley).
Comments: New from ADR 0004 review (2026-07-20). Fixture built 2026-07-22.

Claim: Social role may activate preventively when tone trajectory is deteriorating, before the classifier labels the thread conflictive (ADR 0004).
Test Setup: Fixture `preventive_social_activation` (evals/fixtures/threads.py, added 2026-07-22, source=synthetic).
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard or `eval-models` with key `preventive_social_activation`; check whether social role activates pre-emptively.
Expected Result: Social role activates despite a non-conflictive state label.
Comments: New from ADR 0004 review (2026-07-20). Fixture built 2026-07-22. Distinct from B09/RQ1 (does trajectory reach the agent at all) - this tests early activation specifically.

Claim: Classifier must abstain under genuinely ambiguous/mixed signals rather than emit a low-confidence intervention (ADR 0008, Principles 3-4).
Test Setup: Fixture `ambiguous_signals` (evals/fixtures/threads.py, added 2026-07-22, source=synthetic).
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard or `eval-models` with key `ambiguous_signals`, across the model roster; check for abstention vs. a forced low-confidence call.
Expected Result: should_intervene=False despite ambiguous signals; no low-confidence intervention forced.
Comments: New from ADR 0008 review (2026-07-20). Fixture built 2026-07-22. Complements B22/RQ12 (which measures confidence-vs-assertion correlation, not the abstention decision itself).

Claim: Dual-state threads (e.g. simultaneously stalled AND off_topic) - the classifier's single-label priority logic is undocumented and may differ across models (ADR 0015).
Test Setup: Fixture `dual_state_stalled_off_topic` (evals/fixtures/threads.py, added 2026-07-22, source=synthetic) - distinct from B14's hostile_then_silent (stalled+conflictive).
Test Description: Trigger via dashboard or `eval-models` with key `dual_state_stalled_off_topic` across multiple models; check whether the priority choice is consistent.
Expected Result: A defensible single state is chosen consistently across models; inconsistency is characterized if found.
Comments: New from ADR 0015 review (2026-07-20). Fixture built 2026-07-22. Same shape as B14 but for a different state pair the ADR explicitly flags as undetected today.

Claim: The five role personas (organizational, intellectual, social, affective, moderator) must produce observably distinct text for the same input thread (ADR 0009).
Test Setup: The same fixture/context run through all 5 role agents (forcing role selection per agent rather than letting the orchestrator choose).
Test Description: Run all 5 role agents against the same input and compare output stylistically (lexical features or LLM-judge).
Expected Result: Measurable stylistic distinction between personas for identical input.
Comments: New from ADR 0009 review (2026-07-20). Distinct from ADR 0020's LLM-judge rubric (C05), which evaluates quality per response, not persona differentiation across roles.

Claim: A non-moderator role that selects the instructor_escalation technique must be detected if it never calls flag_content - currently an undetected gap (ADR 0025).
Test Setup: Bias or force a non-moderator role to select instructor_escalation (e.g. via a conflictive-adjacent fixture where escalation is plausible but not moderator-exclusive).
Test Description: Run the scenario, check whether the system currently flags the technique/action mismatch (declared technique vs. executed tool call).
Expected Result: Either the mismatch is caught and reported, or it's confirmed undetected and logged as a known gap.
Comments: New from ADR 0025 review (2026-07-20) - high value: tests whether declared technique matches executed action, distinct from A10 (which only tests the moderator's own correct path).

Claim: RQ5/RQ7 - How much does the technique-repertoire ordering bias (ADR 0016) move technique selection - and do weaker/smaller models show more ordering-bias-driven skew than stronger ones?
Test Setup: Requires a CODE CHANGE: temporarily shuffle or reverse the fixed TECHNIQUES tuple order in discussion_moderation/agents/knowledge_base.py before running.
Test Description: Re-run the 8 fixtures with the shuffled order across the full model roster, compare technique-selection distribution per role per model against the current fixed-order baseline; cross-reference shift magnitude against model size/family.
Expected Result: If ordering bias is real, technique selection distribution shifts measurably when order changes; if weaker models are more susceptible, their shift is larger.
Environment: local (cheap to run broadly before trying idril/EC2)
Comments: Merges RQ5 and RQ7 - RQ7 is a cross-reference of RQ5's own results against model size/family; no separate run needed.

Claim: RQ6 - Does the schema-echo failure mode correlate with extraction mode? model family? or model size?
Test Setup: None new - this is a write-up task over existing/fresh result data, not a new run.
Test Description: Cross-tabulate extraction mode x model family x schema-echo rate from docs/experiments/results/.
Expected Result: A clear table showing whether schema-echo clusters by mode, family, or size - or none of those.
Comments: See docs/experiments/research-questions.md for full literature/ADR grounding.

Claim: RQ12 - What is the actual agreement rate between the structural-assertion layer (ADR 0020 Layer 1) and self-reported confidence (ADR 0028)?
Test Setup: None new - correlate existing fields from fresh run data once available.
Test Description: Correlate confidence values against assertion pass/fail across the fresh idril/EC2/local campaign.
Expected Result: A clear correlation (or lack of one) reported, not asserted.
Comments: See docs/experiments/research-questions.md for full literature/ADR grounding.

Claim: RQ15 - How much of the historical model-comparison data was quietly wrong because of the empty-string error bug (fixed 2026-07-20)?
Test Setup: None new, but moot for the upcoming campaign - fresh runs use the fixed code from the start. Relevant only for historical docs/experiments/results/ summaries generated before the fix.
Test Description: Re-derive error_count/completion_count for pre-fix historical runs using the now-fixed normalize_error/thread_view logic.
Expected Result: Identify which historical summary tables/writeups need correcting.
Comments: See docs/experiments/research-questions.md for full literature/ADR grounding.

Claim: Baseline comparison (ADR 0020, 'comparación con línea base'): does the multi-agent pipeline actually add value over a single-prompt facilitation response from the same underlying model?
Test Setup: Same model run two ways on the same fixture: through the full pipeline vs. a single direct prompt asking for a facilitation response with no orchestration/retry/rule-check layers.
Test Description: Generate both outputs for a representative subset of fixtures, compare structurally (does the baseline even satisfy Layer 1 assertions) and qualitatively.
Expected Result: Documents the delta the pipeline architecture contributes over the model's raw capability - the most direct evidence for 'is this architecture worth it.'
Comments: New (2026-07-20) - ADR 0020 calls for this comparison but no existing row covered it. Central to the thesis's core architecture-feasibility claim.

Claim: Generated responses must be scored for pedagogical/technical quality by a model distinct from every evaluated model (ADR 0020 Layer 2).
Test Setup: Rubric implementation (six dimensions: adequacy to state, technique fidelity, non-evaluative language, grounding, action alignment, tone). Judge model: Claude, since claude-sonnet-4.5 was removed from EC2's EVAL_MODELS this session specifically so Claude never judges its own output.
Test Description: Score existing Layer-1-passing (model, thread, response) triples from the fresh idril/EC2 campaign against the rubric.
Expected Result: Each triple gets a per-dimension score with justification.
Environment: N/A (once implemented, run against fresh result data)
Comments: Blocked on rubric implementation, not on the judge-model decision (resolved).

Claim: The LLM judge must be calibrated against human expert judgment (ADR 0020 Layer 3).
Test Setup: Two annotators with academic facilitation background (not yet recruited).
Test Description: Annotate 20-30 (thread, response) pairs with the same rubric; compute Cohen's kappa; resolve cases below 0.6 by consensus.
Expected Result: A calibration reference for the automated judge, with inter-annotator agreement reported.
Comments: Open question: sample only Layer-1 successes, or include cases the automated judge scores low? Blocked on recruiting annotators.
